data.title: Rex International Holds Extraordinary General Meeting with Hybrid Attendance
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Rex International Holding Ltd. ( (SG:5WH) ) has shared an announcement.

Rex International Holding Ltd. convened an extraordinary general meeting in Singapore on 28 January 2026, chaired by Lead Independent Director Pong Chen Yih on behalf of the board chairman, who attended remotely. The meeting confirmed the presence of a quorum, introduced attending board and management members both in person and via Zoom, and adopted the meeting notice with the chairman also serving as proxy for certain shareholders in line with their voting instructions.

The EGM’s orderly conduct and hybrid attendance format underscored Rex’s adherence to corporate governance norms and shareholder engagement practices. By formally recording quorum, directorship roles and proxy voting arrangements, the company reinforced procedural transparency and proper oversight of its decision-making processes.

The most recent analyst rating on (SG:5WH) stock is a Hold with a S$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rex International Holding Ltd. stock, see the SG:5WH Stock Forecast page.

More about Rex International Holding Ltd.

Rex International Holding Ltd. is an oil and gas exploration and production company incorporated in Singapore. The company focuses on upstream energy assets and is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent directors, supported by key management personnel in finance and communications.

Average Trading Volume: 12,676,622

data.title: Sandon Capital Names New Director Kirsten Hannan With No Initial Equity Stake
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sandon Capital Investments Limited ( (AU:SNC) ) has provided an announcement.

Sandon Capital Investments Limited has appointed Kirsten Jane Hannan as a director effective 20 February 2026, according to an initial director’s interest notice lodged with the ASX. At the time of appointment, Hannan holds no relevant interests in the company’s securities and has no interests in any related contracts, indicating she joins the board without an equity stake or associated contractual ties.

The disclosure underscores the company’s compliance with ASX governance and transparency requirements around director appointments. For investors and other stakeholders, the notice clarifies that any future alignment of Hannan’s interests with shareholders, such as through share acquisitions or incentive arrangements, would occur after her appointment and be separately disclosed.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SNC) stock is a Buy with a A$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sandon Capital Investments Limited stock, see the AU:SNC Stock Forecast page.

More about Sandon Capital Investments Limited

Sandon Capital Investments Limited is an Australian listed investment company. It operates as a closed‑end fund, providing investors with exposure to a portfolio of listed securities, and is subject to ASX listing rules and Australian corporate governance requirements.

data.title: ASX Extends Suspension of 3D Energi Over Disclosure Failures
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from 3D Oil Limited ( (AU:TDO) ).

3D Energi Limited remains suspended from quotation on the ASX after failing to provide a required market update on its funding position and the impact of a payment default on its interest in the VIC/P79 exploration permit. The ASX has converted the suspension into an exchange-imposed halt under Listing Rule 17.3 and will not reinstate trading until it is satisfied that the company complies with its continuous disclosure obligations and broader listing requirements.

The extended suspension heightens uncertainty for shareholders and signals regulatory concerns over the adequacy and timeliness of 3D Energi’s disclosure practices. It also raises questions about the security of the company’s stake in a key exploration asset and may affect market confidence in its financial resilience and operational continuity.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:TDO) stock is a Hold with a A$0.08 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on 3D Oil Limited stock, see the AU:TDO Stock Forecast page.

More about 3D Oil Limited

3D Energi Limited, listed on the ASX under the code TDO, operates in the energy sector with a focus on oil and gas exploration. The company holds interests in offshore exploration permits, including the VIC/P79 permit, which is a key asset shaping its exploration portfolio and future growth prospects.

Average Trading Volume: 3,277,926

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$41.94M

Frontier Management, Inc. ( (JP:7038) ) has shared an update.

Frontier Management Inc. has nominated four candidates for director positions, including two new directors and two incumbents, to be voted on at its 19th Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders on March 26, 2026. The move is aimed at revitalizing the management structure as the current terms of several directors end.

Deputy President and Executive Officer Sumio Nishizawa and Managing Executive Officer Kyogo Murase have been nominated as new directors, reflecting their track records in driving sales, winning large projects, and leading company-wide business reforms. Their elevation to the board underscores Frontier Management’s focus on strengthening governance and execution capabilities to support medium- to long-term growth and enhance corporate value for stakeholders.

data.title: FirstCape Group Builds 5% Substantial Stake in Spark New Zealand
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spark New Zealand Limited ( (AU:SPK) ) has issued an announcement.

Spark New Zealand has disclosed that FirstCape Group Limited has become a substantial product holder in the company, accumulating a 5.06% stake comprising 95,643,717 ordinary shares. The position is held through several wholly owned subsidiaries, including Harbour Asset Management, BNZ Investment Services, and JBWere (NZ), which collectively exercise voting and investment management powers on behalf of underlying clients.

The new substantial holding underscores continued institutional interest in Spark’s equity and further concentrates ownership among professional asset managers. This development may enhance liquidity in Spark’s shares and signals ongoing confidence from large investors in the company’s long-term prospects within New Zealand’s telecommunications sector.

More about Spark New Zealand Limited

Spark New Zealand Limited is a major telecommunications and digital services provider in New Zealand, offering mobile, fixed-line, broadband, and data services to consumers, businesses, and public sector clients. The company operates in a highly competitive market, where institutional investors and asset managers play a significant role in its ownership structure and market valuation.

Recent Updates on Amazon.Com, Inc. stock

Amazon shares have been volatile, sliding as much as the mid‑teens percentage over recent weeks before a modest rebound, and currently trade well below Wall Street’s Strong Buy 12‑month targets in the high‑$280s to near $300, implying sizable upside. Analysts say Q4 results beat expectations with AWS revenue up 24% and retail margins strong, but guidance for roughly $200 billion of capex through 2026, Project Leo spending, and weaker near‑term free cash flow are pressuring the stock even as AWS, AI, advertising, and logistics investments are expected to drive faster growth and higher earnings beyond 2026.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AMZN is a Neutral.

The score is led by strong financial performance (margin expansion and improving balance-sheet leverage) and a generally positive earnings call outlook (AWS/AI acceleration and strong backlog).
